First-aid inspired healthcare workspace for patients, providers, and care coordinators

Every core module has its own access space in one connected response board

Web Mobile USSD 24/7 triage Alerts

Care Navigation Platform

CAREPATH FIRST RESPONSE

A modular patient-access board where symptom intake, AI triage, first-aid capture, telemedicine, cost visibility, appointment booking, follow-up, low-bandwidth access, notifications, and records live in distinct spaces but move as one care journey.

Open AI Triage View First Aid Hub
01AI Triage 02First Aid Hub 03Facility Matching 04Telemedicine 05Cost and Payments 06Appointments 07Follow-Up 08Low-Bandwidth Access 09Notifications 10EHR and Prescriptions 11Care Network

01 AI Triage

Describe symptoms and get urgency guidance fast

Patients enter symptoms and receive likely conditions, urgency level, and next-step recommendations.

Access AI Triage

02 First Aid Hub

Emergency guidance, documentation, and video space in one module

This workspace keeps everything for first response inside one environment so the user does not leave the hub to explore key accident guidance.

Open Video Spaces Read Documentation

First Aid Field

Capture the emergency before routing the patient onward

Responders can log the incident, record the patient state, and pass the details forward into triage, telemedicine, or urgent referral without losing context.

Cuts and bleeding Burns and scalds Falls and fractures Choking Road accidents

Case 01

Cuts and heavy bleeding

Use this when a person has a deep cut, visible bleeding, or a wound that may not stop on its own.

  • Apply firm direct pressure with a clean cloth or bandage.
  • Raise the injured area if possible and safe.
  • Keep pressure on until bleeding slows or help arrives.
  • Seek urgent care for severe bleeding or large wounds.
Video Space Bleeding control tutorial placeholder

Case 02

Burns and scalds

For injuries caused by hot liquids, open flame, hot metal, or electrical contact.

  • Cool the burn under clean running water for at least 20 minutes.
  • Remove tight items near the burn before swelling begins.
  • Cover loosely with a clean non-stick dressing or cloth.
  • Do not apply butter, oil, or ice directly to the burn.
Video Space Burn cooling and dressing walkthrough placeholder

Case 03

Falls, sprains, and suspected fractures

Use this for slips, falls, twisted joints, or pain with swelling and limited movement.

  • Keep the injured person still and support the injured area.
  • Apply a wrapped cold pack to reduce swelling.
  • Do not force a limb back into place or ask them to walk.
  • Get urgent care if there is severe pain, deformity, or head impact.
Video Space Fracture support and safe immobilization placeholder

Case 04

Choking and blocked airway

For adults or children who cannot speak, cough, or breathe properly because something is blocking the airway.

  • Encourage coughing if the person can still breathe.
  • Give back blows and abdominal thrusts when airway blockage is severe.
  • Call emergency help immediately if breathing stops.
  • Start CPR if the person becomes unresponsive and is not breathing.
Video Space Choking response demonstration placeholder

Case 05

Road traffic accidents and head injury

For crashes, collisions, or any situation where neck, spine, or head trauma may be present.

  • Call emergency services and keep the person still.
  • Do not remove helmets unless breathing is blocked and you must intervene.
  • Control visible bleeding with gentle direct pressure if safe.
  • Monitor breathing and consciousness until trained help arrives.
Video Space Road accident first response placeholder

03 Facility Matching

Find the right hospital or clinician nearby

Match patients to capability, availability, distance, and required level of care.

Find Care Options

04 Telemedicine

Start a virtual consultation when travel can wait

Give users a direct route into chat or video consultations for non-emergency cases.

Start Teleconsult

05 Cost and Payments

See cost expectations before committing to care

Estimate treatment expense, surface payment options, and keep M-Pesa ready as part of the decision.

Check Estimated Cost

06 Appointments

Book the next step without leaving the platform

Schedule in-person visits or specialist slots after triage and matching are complete.

Book Appointment

07 Follow-Up

Keep recovery, reminders, and review visits in one place

Follow-up gets its own access space for check-ins, medication reminders, care plans, and post-visit monitoring.

Open Follow-Up Care

08 Low-Bandwidth Access

Keep care available through USSD, SMS, and lightweight mobile flows

The platform supports rural and unstable-network journeys through simple channels built for low connectivity and time-limited sessions.

Open Access Channels

09 Notifications

Send reminders, booking updates, and urgent care alerts

SMS, push, and email keep patients, providers, and facilities aligned across booking, consultation, referral, and follow-up.

View Alerts and Reminders

10 EHR and Prescriptions

Capture consultations, diagnoses, and treatment history

Maintain continuity across providers by keeping health records and prescriptions connected.

Open Patient Records

11 Care Network

Labs, pharmacies, hospitals, and alerts stay synchronized

Integrations support referrals, diagnostics, prescription fulfillment, and notifications across the full journey.

View Connected Services

Core Access Spaces

Every major product capability is presented as its own module

AI triage First aid field and guidance First aid videos and docs Facility matching Telemedicine Cost and M-Pesa Appointments Follow-up care USSD and SMS access Notifications and reminders EHR and prescriptions Labs, pharmacies, notifications
